title: Modi’s Mega Coimbatore Roadshow Draws Massive NDA Crowds Ahead of April 23 Polls
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi led a 6-km roadshow across Avinashi Road and Gandhipuram, energising NDA cadres in the Kongu capital just three days before polling. Local leaders said the turnout was the largest in a decade.

title: Senthilbalaji Promises ‘Global City’ Push for Coimbatore South
summary: DMK candidate V. Senthilbalaji wrapped his door-to-door blitz in Singanallur with promises of a fully-funded Global City masterplan, metro phase-2 acceleration and a dedicated MSME relief fund.

title: Coimbatore EV Component Makers Secure $80M Series-B Funding
summary: Three home-grown EV component startups headquartered in Peelamedu and Saravanampatti closed a combined $80 million Series-B round, reinforcing Coimbatore’s claim as the ‘Detroit of South India’.

title: Valankulam Lake Rejuvenation Phase-3 Opens to Public
summary: The Smart City Mission unveiled the third phase of Valankulam restoration with new walking trails, biodiversity zones and night-lighting, integrating 14 acres of public space.

title: New Cardiac & Neuro Block at CMCH Becomes Fully Operational
summary: Coimbatore Government Medical College Hospital’s multispecialty block began full operations today, offering free advanced cardiac and neurological care to the underprivileged.

title: Safe-City AI Cameras Help Bust Cyber-Fraud Ring Targeting Senior Citizens
summary: City police dismantled a 12-member cyber-fraud gang using leads from the 5,000-camera AI command centre. Over ₹4.2 crore allegedly siphoned from elderly residents has been frozen.

title: Flying Squads Seize ₹62 Lakh Unaccounted Cash in Pollachi
summary: Election flying squads intercepted two vehicles near Pollachi bypass and seized ₹62 lakh in unaccounted cash along with campaign material in violation of MCC.

title: Home-Voting Begins for 11,400 Elderly and PwD Voters Across District
summary: Polling teams started door-step voting for senior citizens above 85 and persons with disabilities across all 10 Coimbatore constituencies under the ECI ‘Vote from Home’ initiative.

title: Valparai Ghat Road Accident: 9 Killed as Tourist Van Falls into Gorge
summary: A tourist van from Kerala plunged into a deep gorge near Valparai, killing nine people including school teachers. PM Modi and President Murmu expressed grief. Bodies handed over after postmortem.

title: Man Attempts Self-Immolation Near Gandhipuram Bus Stand, Critical
summary: A 45-year-old man set himself ablaze near Gandhipuram Bus Stand and is battling for life at Coimbatore Medical College Hospital with 72% burns. Police are investigating the motive.

title: Kamal Haasan Campaigns for DMK’s Senthilbalaji in Coimbatore South
summary: MNM chief Kamal Haasan addressed a rally in Coimbatore South, urging voters to support DMK candidate V. Senthilbalaji and recalling his own 2021 electoral contest in the city.

title: NDA’s Chandrababu Naidu to Begin 2-Day Campaign from Coimbatore
summary: Andhra Pradesh CM N. Chandrababu Naidu will kick off a two-day NDA campaign tour from Coimbatore tomorrow, aiming to consolidate alliance votes ahead of April 23 polling.

title: Triangular Fight Intensifies in Coimbatore North
summary: Coimbatore North has turned into a three-way battle between BJP’s Vanathi Srinivasan, DMK, and TVK candidates, with door-to-door campaigns reaching a fever pitch.

title: 30 Held, 1,730 LPG Cylinders Seized in Hoarding Crackdown
summary: Civil supplies-CID police arrested 30 individuals for hoarding commercial LPG cylinders, seizing 1,730 cylinders and 14 vehicles across the district.

title: Acid Attack Over Money Dispute in R.S. Puram
summary: A man was arrested for throwing acid on his roommate following a financial dispute near R.S. Puram. The victim is undergoing treatment at a private hospital.

title: Corporation Bill Collector Held for ₹25,000 Bribe
summary: A bill collector attached to the Coimbatore Corporation was caught red-handed accepting a bribe of ₹25,000 to hand over property tax books.

title: New ₹13 Crore Bus Terminal Inaugurated at Ukkadam
summary: Coimbatore’s new Ukkadam bus terminal, built at ₹13 crore, features 28 bays, modern waiting halls, and digital display boards. Two terminals are planned at a total cost of ₹21.55 crore.

title: NDR InvIT Acquires Two Warehousing Assets in Coimbatore for ₹260 Cr
summary: NDR InvIT Trust has acquired two Grade-A warehousing facilities in Coimbatore, signaling the city’s rapid growth as a logistics and industrial hub.

title: Fire at Vellalore Dump Yard Extinguished After 4 Hours
summary: A major fire broke out at the Vellalore dump yard but was brought under control within four hours. CCTV footage is being examined for possible foul play.

title: New UG Courses in Visual Communication, Biochemistry Proposed for Govt Colleges
summary: A committee reviewed feasibility of introducing Visual Communication and Biochemistry courses in government colleges across the Coimbatore region.

title: 85 Govt Schools in Coimbatore Get AI, Robotics Training Under TN SPARK
summary: Under the Tamil Nadu SPARK initiative, 85 government schools in the district are now training students in artificial intelligence, coding, and robotics.

title: Candlelight Tribute to A.R. Rahman & S.P. Balasubrahmanyam at Art Gallery
summary: An intimate candlelight concert series begins tonight at Kasthuri Sreenivasan Trust Art Gallery, featuring tributes to legendary musicians A.R. Rahman and S.P. Balasubrahmanyam.

title: Mirchi Live Concert with Stephen Devassy Scheduled in Coimbatore
summary: A musical fiesta featuring keyboard maestro Stephen Devassy and other artists is set to take place at a city venue next week.

title: Coimbatore Weather: High of 32°C, Light Rain Expected
summary: The city will see a maximum temperature of 32°C with clear skies and light rain likely in the morning hours. Humidity levels remain moderate.

title: Heavy Traffic Curbs on Avinashi Road Due to Political Rallies
summary: Traffic restrictions are in place on Avinashi Road and near the airport following high-profile political visits. Commuters are advised to use alternative routes.

title: Tender Floated for Industrial Plot with Factory Building in Coimbatore
summary: A government tender for an industrial plot along with a factory building has been announced, with a submission deadline of April 20.

title: KSRTC Premium Bus Service Between Pathanamthitta and Coimbatore Discontinued
summary: KSRTC’s premium bus services connecting Pathanamthitta to Coimbatore have been discontinued temporarily due to engine issues, affecting regular passengers.

title: CM Stalin: Coimbatore Will Become DMK Stronghold in 2026
summary: Chief Minister M.K. Stalin asserted that Coimbatore will turn into a DMK stronghold in the current elections and expressed confidence in the secular progressive alliance's victory.

title: Elderly Woman’s Body Found in Gorge Near Valparai, Partially Eaten by Animal
summary: A 76-year-old woman was found dead in a gorge near Valparai, with the corpse partially consumed by an unidentified carnivore. Police have registered a case.

title: Coimbatore Railway Station Parking Scam Exposed
summary: A private contractor at Coimbatore Junction was found charging illegal parking fees of ₹40 for two-wheelers. Passengers have been asked to report overcharging.

title: Race Course Gets 100% Voter Turnout Hoardings
summary: Bold hoardings promoting 100% voter turnout have been installed at Race Course as part of SVEEP activities ahead of April 23 polling.

title: Postal Voting Continues for Service Voters in Coimbatore
summary: Postal ballot distribution for service voters and polling personnel is progressing smoothly across all 10 assembly constituencies.

title: ₹5.97 Crore Seized So Far by Flying Squads in Coimbatore
summary: Election flying squads have seized cash and valuables worth ₹5.97 crore to date. Of this, ₹5.06 crore was returned after proper documentation.

title: Two Ballot Units Per Booth in 5 Constituencies
summary: Mettupalayam, Coimbatore North, Thondamuthur, Coimbatore South and Singanallur will use two ballot units per polling station due to a high number of candidates.

title: Home Voting Facility for Senior Citizens Underway
summary: Polling teams are visiting residences of citizens aged 85+ and persons with disabilities across Coimbatore to facilitate home voting.

title: DMK Functionaries Booked for Distributing ₹8,000 Gift Coupons
summary: Police booked DMK functionaries for allegedly distributing gift coupons worth ₹8,000 in Coimbatore. BJP’s Annamalai termed it organised vote-buying.

title: ₹10 Crore Jewellery Seized During Pre-Poll Checks
summary: Election surveillance teams seized gold, diamond, and silver jewellery valued at ₹10 crore during vehicle checks in the district.

title: Delhi CM Rekha Gupta Campaigns for Vanathi Srinivasan
summary: Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta addressed women’s gatherings in Coimbatore North, endorsing BJP candidate Vanathi Srinivasan and stressing women’s empowerment.

title: EPS Reassures Voters on Delimitation at Coimbatore Rally
summary: AIADMK leader Edappadi K. Palaniswami addressed a rally in Coimbatore, dismissing CM Stalin’s concerns over the delimitation bill and calling it a non-issue.

title: P Chidambaram Warns of Reduced Southern Influence in Parliament
summary: Senior Congress leader P. Chidambaram told reporters in Coimbatore that delimitation would suppress South India’s voice in the Lok Sabha.

title: 183 Candidates in Fray Across 10 Coimbatore Seats
summary: A total of 183 candidates are contesting the assembly elections across Coimbatore’s 10 constituencies, with Coimbatore South seeing the highest at 31.

title: Vanathi Srinivasan Declares ₹13 Crore Assets
summary: BJP’s Coimbatore North candidate Vanathi Srinivasan has declared family assets worth over ₹13 crore in her election affidavit.

title: Senthilbalaji Declares ₹4.54 Crore Assets
summary: DMK’s Coimbatore South candidate V. Senthilbalaji and family have declared total assets of ₹4.54 crore.

title: S P Velumani’s Family Assets Rise to ₹13.08 Crore
summary: AIADMK candidate from Thondamuthur, S P Velumani, showed an increase in family assets to ₹13.08 crore in his affidavit.

title: TNAU Grants Extended Holidays for Student Voting
summary: Tamil Nadu Agricultural University has extended holidays for students to enable them to vote in their home constituencies on April 23.

title: ‘Run for Vote’ Draws 350+ Participants at Race Course
summary: Over 350 runners took part in the ‘Run for Vote’ event at Race Course, pledging to achieve 100% voter turnout in their wards.

title: EVM-VVPAT Commissioning Completed in All Constituencies
summary: Commissioning of EVMs and VVPATs with mock polls has been completed across Coimbatore in the presence of candidate agents.

title: 24x7 Election Control Room Active – Toll Free 1800-425-0086
summary: Coimbatore district’s election control room is operational round the clock. Citizens can lodge complaints regarding MCC violations or voter issues.

title: Senthilbalaji Highlights ₹760 Crore Road Allocation
summary: Campaigning in Coimbatore South, DMK candidate Senthilbalaji reminded voters of the ₹760 crore allocated by the DMK government for city road development.

title: Annamalai Predicts ‘Huge NDA Mandate’ in Western TN
summary: BJP state president K. Annamalai, campaigning in Coimbatore, predicted a massive mandate for the NDA alliance in the western region.

title: 8,343 Ballot Units & 5,572 VVPATs Ready for Polling Day
summary: Coimbatore district is fully equipped with 8,343 ballot units and 5,572 VVPATs for the April 23 assembly election.

title: IT Raids Conducted at Textile & Jewellery Shops in Coimbatore
summary: Income Tax officials carried out raids at several textile and jewellery showrooms in the city as part of poll-time enforcement.

title: BJP Leaders Perform Silambam During Coimbatore Roadshow
summary: To connect with traditional voters, BJP leaders showcased silambam (traditional martial art) during a roadshow in Coimbatore.

title: District Election Officer Dances with Tribal Voters
summary: In a unique awareness drive, the DEO joined tribal communities in a folk dance to promote 100% voting in rural Coimbatore.

title: Drone Restrictions Imposed Across City Until Polling Day
summary: Authorities have imposed a complete ban on drone flights over Coimbatore to ensure security during the final campaign and polling phases.

title: Colleges Modify Schedules to Boost Student Voting
summary: Several arts and science colleges in Coimbatore have rescheduled internal exams to allow students to travel and vote on April 23.

title: Post-Rally Cleanup Accelerated by Corporation Sanitation Teams
summary: Sanitation workers are working overtime to clear litter from major rally venues across Coimbatore, with special focus on Avinashi Road and Race Course.

title: Coimbatore Stays a Critical Electoral Battleground
summary: Political analysts note that Coimbatore’s 10 seats could determine the overall balance of power in western Tamil Nadu.

title: DMK Intensifies Final-Phase Door-to-Door Outreach
summary: DMK cadres are focusing on first-time voters and welfare scheme beneficiaries in a final push before the silent period begins.

title: SVEEP Street Plays at Bus Stands and Markets
summary: Voter awareness street plays are being performed at Gandhipuram, Ukkadam, and Singanallur bus stands to encourage turnout.

title: Flying Squads Intensify Vehicle Checks on Highways
summary: Checkpoints at L&T Bypass, Mettupalayam Road, and Pollachi Highway have been strengthened to curb illegal cash and liquor movement.

title: Postal Ballot Distribution Progressing Smoothly
summary: Officials confirmed that postal ballots have been dispatched to service voters and are being collected without delays.

title: AIADMK Holds Booth-Level Worker Meetings
summary: AIADMK convened meetings for polling booth agents across Coimbatore to finalise voter mobilisation plans.

title: Colleges Host Voter Pledge Programs with Mock EVMs
summary: Students at PSG Tech, Kumaraguru College, and GRD College took voter pledges and practiced on mock EVMs.

title: Static Surveillance Teams Report Daily to Observers
summary: Static teams across Coimbatore are providing daily reports on cash, liquor, and freebie movement to election observers.

title: Strong Rooms Sealed Under Multi-Tier Security
summary: EVM strong rooms at government colleges and ITI campuses have been sealed with CCTV and paramilitary cover.

title: BJP Highlights Central Schemes in Roadshow
summary: BJP candidates highlighted Ayushman Bharat, PM Awas Yojana, and Ujjwala during roadshows in Singanallur and Sulur.

title: Booth Slip Distribution Crosses 80% Mark
summary: Booth-level officers have distributed voter slips to over 80% of households, aiming for full coverage by April 21.

title: MCC Notices Issued for Unauthorised Hoardings
summary: The election commission issued notices to remove over 150 unauthorised hoardings and banners across the city.

title: Collector Reviews Polling Station Readiness
summary: District Collector inspected polling stations in rural areas to ensure drinking water, ramps, and lighting are in place.

title: TVK Cadre Steps Up Door-to-Door Campaign
summary: TVK volunteers distributed manifestos and pamphlets in Kavundampalayam and Peelamedu, focusing on local issues.

title: Voter Helpline Receives Record Calls from Coimbatore
summary: The state voter helpline logged over 1,200 calls from Coimbatore residents in the last 24 hours, mostly seeking polling booth locations.

title: Liquor Worth ₹1.2 Crore Seized in Coimbatore
summary: Election squads seized a large consignment of illicit liquor near the Kerala border and at Madukkarai checkpost.

title: Final Voter List Verification Concluded
summary: BLOs completed the final round of voter list verification, deleting duplicate entries and adding new eligible voters.

title: NTK Organises Cycle Rally Across Coimbatore Towns
summary: Naam Tamilar Katchi conducted a cycle rally from Mettupalayam to Pollachi, raising environmental and language issues.

title: Differently-Abled Voters Get Dedicated Transport
summary: The district administration has arranged free buses and wheelchairs for differently-abled voters on polling day.

title: Income Tax Teams Conduct Discreet Surveys
summary: IT teams surveyed high-value transactions in jewellery and real estate shops to track undisclosed election spending.

title: Sector Officers Deployed Across Polling Zones
summary: A total of 45 sector officers have been deployed to monitor polling booths and address real-time issues on April 23.

title: Congress Workers Hold Street-Corner Meetings
summary: Congress alliance workers held small gatherings in Kovaipudur and Vadavalli, promising education and healthcare reforms.

title: Social Media Influencers Run #CoimbatoreVotes Campaign
summary: Local Instagram and YouTube creators are posting reels and shorts urging youth to vote on April 23.

title: Cybercrime Cell Monitors Election Misinformation
summary: The Coimbatore cybercrime cell has flagged 20+ social media posts for spreading fake news about polling dates and EVMs.

title: Election Control Room Operates Round the Clock
summary: The control room at the Collectorate is handling complaints related to MCC violations, campaign noise, and voter ID issues.

title: Coimbatore Corporation Steps Up Waste Collection
summary: Due to increased litter from rallies, the Corporation has deployed additional compactors and door-to-door collection vans.

title: Flyover Work at Ramanathapuram Junction Nears Completion
summary: The long-pending flyover project at Ramanathapuram is 85% complete and expected to open by June 2026.

title: CMCH Conducts Free Mega Health Camp at Sungam
summary: Coimbatore Medical College Hospital organised a free health check-up camp at Sungam, screening over 800 residents for diabetes and hypertension.

title: Coimbatore Marathon Scheduled for May 3
summary: The annual Coimbatore Marathon has been announced for May 3, with categories for full, half, and 10K runs. Registrations are open.

title: Noyyal River Cleanup Drive by Youth Groups
summary: Over 200 volunteers participated in a Noyyal river cleanup drive near Singanallur, removing plastic and invasive weeds.

title: Coimbatore Textile Exporters See 12% Growth in Q1 2026
summary: The Coimbatore Tirupur textile cluster reported a 12% rise in exports during the first quarter, driven by demand from European markets.

title: Gang Stealing Two-Wheelers Busted in Saibaba Colony
summary: Police arrested a three-member gang involved in stealing over 20 two-wheelers from Saibaba Colony and nearby areas.

title: New Foot Overbridge at Gandhipuram Gets Green Light
summary: The Corporation approved a new foot overbridge at Gandhipuram to ease pedestrian congestion near the bus stand.

title: CBSE Schools in Coimbatore Announce Summer Vacation from May 2
summary: Most CBSE-affiliated schools in the city will close for summer break on May 2, reopening on June 8.

title: NTK Candidate Files Complaint Against Rival for MCC Violation
summary: NTK’s Coimbatore North candidate lodged a complaint alleging distribution of cash by an opposition party worker.

title: Silent Period to Begin from April 21 Midnight
summary: Campaigning will cease at 6 PM on April 21, after which no public meetings or processions will be allowed.

title: Posters of ‘Missed Call’ Voter Helpline Installed at Bus Stops
summary: Posters displaying a missed call number for voter information have been put up at over 100 bus stops across Coimbatore.

title: Paramilitary Forces Conduct Flag March in Coimbatore
summary: Central paramilitary forces carried out a flag march in sensitive areas of the city to instil confidence among voters.

title: Coimbatore Records 78% Voter Turnout in 2021 – Target 85% in 2026
summary: Officials aim to surpass the previous 78% turnout by encouraging urban voters through targeted awareness campaigns.

title: Coimbatore Vizha 2026 to Be Held in June
summary: The annual Coimbatore Vizha celebrating art, food, and culture is scheduled for the first weekend of June at CODISSIA.

title: One-Way System Introduced Near Brookefields Mall
summary: To ease congestion, the traffic police have introduced a one-way system on the service road near Brookefields Mall.

title: Property Registrations Drop 15% in March Due to Election Code
summary: The model code of conduct led to a temporary dip in property registrations, but activity is expected to resume post-elections.

title: Dengue Prevention Drives Intensified in Coimbatore
summary: Corporation teams are conducting fogging and larva destruction drives in wards with reported dengue cases.

title: Coimbatore Startups Win ₹5 Cr Funding at TiE Conclave
summary: Two city-based agritech startups secured ₹5 crore in funding at the TiE Coimbatore Entrepreneurship Conclave.

title: Chain Snatcher Arrested After High-Speed Chase in Peelamedu
summary: Police arrested a bike-borne chain snatcher following a chase near Peelamedu flyover. Stolen gold recovered.

title: Underground Drainage Work Completed in 15 New Wards
summary: The Corporation announced completion of underground drainage work in 15 wards, benefiting over 10,000 households.

title: Anna University Coimbatore Releases Exam Schedule
summary: Anna University’s Coimbatore regional campus released the end-semester exam schedule for engineering courses, starting May 5.

title: Woman Candidate from Coimbatore South Raises Safety Issues
summary: An independent woman candidate in Coimbatore South filed a complaint about alleged threats from rival supporters.

title: Coimbatore Has 28.5 Lakh Eligible Voters
summary: The district has a total of 28.5 lakh registered voters, including 14.2 lakh women and 2,300 third-gender voters.

title: Auto Rickshaws Display ‘Vote Karo’ Stickers
summary: Over 5,000 auto rickshaws in Coimbatore now carry voter awareness stickers as part of a transport department initiative.

title: Election Observer Holds Meeting with Bank Managers
summary: The election observer instructed bank managers to report suspicious cash withdrawals above ₹10 lakh.

title: Coimbatore Gets 200 New Dustbins at Public Places
summary: The Corporation installed 200 new dustbins at bus stops, parks, and markets to tackle littering during the election period.

title: Evening Thunderstorms Likely in Coimbatore on April 20
summary: The IMD has forecast evening thunderstorms with gusty winds for April 20, advising outdoor event organisers to take precautions.
